name: Jamaican Rice & Peas
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan with a tight-fitting lid, place the washed beans.
2. Add the coconut milk and bring to boil.
3. Reduce heat. Cook until the beans are tender, 1 to 2 hours.
4. Add thyme, garlic, green onion, and salt. Add hot pepper if desired.
5. Simmer for a few minutes.
6. Add rice. The liquid should come up to 1-inch above the rice. Add water if needed.
7. Cover the pot. Bring to a boil again.
8. Reduce heat to low. Cook for 15 minutes (for regular rice) to 20 minutes (for parboiled).
9. Rice is ready when the liquid disappears and the grains of rice are separate. Stir just before serving to evenly distribute the red kidney beans.
10. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
NOTE: For coconut milk, crack 1 mature coconut with a hammer, cut off the brown shell, and grate the coconut meat. Into a bowl, add the coconut meat and 6 cups of water. Mix well, then squeeze to extract as much liquid as possible. Press through a sieve and use as directed above.
